features.name: ADAS features
verbatim: The ADAS works fantastically well.
my favourite ADAS feature is this one which is LVDS. Sorry, I can't pronounce that. It means leading vehicle departure alert. Basically, it alerts you if the car ahead has started to move already.

features.name: USB connectivity
verbatim: the wired phone projection only works with USB-A, not with USB-C which is really frustrating.

features.name: air purifier
verbatim: The air purifier display was removed in the facelift and the air purifier is now buried deep in the menu.

features.name: ambient lighting
verbatim: There is just a single colour for the ambient lighting when it should have had at least 64.

features.name: audio quality
verbatim: The Bose sound system has good audio quality.

features.name: blind spot monitoring
verbatim: Best of all is the blind view monitor, super convenient and you also have a driver rear view monitor which works at all speeds
the blind view alert and safe exit warning are really useful.

features.name: boot features
verbatim: Under the boot, you will find exposed wires.

features.name: braking performance
verbatim: the brakes because the pedal feel isn't the best and the car doesn't feel sure-footed when you apply heavy brakes because the tyres, they screech in protest.

features.name: climate control
verbatim: dual zone climate control AC.

features.name: connected car features
verbatim: Blue Link is complimentary for the first 3 years post which you have to pay either Rs. 2299 for 1 year extension or Rs. 4349 for 2 year extension.

features.name: cost cutting
verbatim: The second con is the cost cutting.

features.name: display screens
verbatim: these dual 10.25 inch screens are the best in the business.

features.name: drive modes
verbatim: The diesel manual gets no drive mode

features.name: driving feel
verbatim: it's about the driving feel here because this is a diesel manual which means you really enjoy shifting gears

features.name: driving range
verbatim: On a full tank of fuel, this car goes almost 900 km provided you drive with a light foot.

features.name: dynamic indicators
verbatim: Dynamic swipe indicators don't work with hazard, whereas it does in cars like the Scorpio N.

features.name: electric parking brake
verbatim: the electric parking brake, a very good feature as it saves space in the centre console but at times, it doesn't get disengaged as you have to apply the brakes firmly.

features.name: engine performance
verbatim: 115bhp doesn't sound much because it simply isn't but 250Nm of torque means, this car wheel spins like crazy when you turn off the traction control and the stability control system.
goes from 0 to 100 kmph in 11.63 seconds which like I told you isn't very fast and especially if you try to drive this car very fast, you'll realise 115bhp is just not enough.
the engine revs all the way till 5000rpm in neutral and it also reaches 5000rpm in gear as well. That's above 5000rpm, that's almost 5300rpm which is crazy.

features.name: front armrest
verbatim: The front centre armrest doesn't slide

features.name: fuel efficiency
verbatim: this is a Creta diesel manual which returns 20 kmpl which is the first pro of this car that is fuel efficiency.
amazing fuel efficiency for the diesel

features.name: handling
verbatim: decent handling

features.name: infotainment system
verbatim: the infotainment system, Hyundai is one of the few car brands that has managed to crack the UI of car infotainment because this one is super slick, high resolution and never ever hangs unlike the ones in Indian cars at a similar price point.
there are 10 sounds of nature, you can record voice memos and it also displays sports data on the infotainment but I haven't used even one of these features in the past one year.

features.name: instrument cluster
verbatim: instrument cluster which too is amazing in quality and super easy to browse, having 3 different cluster modes, a super useful tyre pressure monitoring system and front and rear parking sensor display.

features.name: interior colour
verbatim: Hyundai should have given options like Kia does but the Creta N-Line has all black interiors which I really like.

features.name: interior materials
verbatim: The fourth con are the hard plastics and the interior colour scheme. It's not to everyone's taste.

features.name: music streaming
verbatim: It has inbuilt Jio Saavan but the Blue Link subscription has expired but how is that possible?

features.name: navigation system
verbatim: The inbuilt navigation is simply lovely, it shows detailed guidance both on the infotainment screen as well as on the instrument cluster which too is amazing in quality and super easy to browse

features.name: parking camera
verbatim: The 360 degree parking camera is of very high quality and has a bird's eye view too.

features.name: parking sensors
verbatim: front and rear parking sensor display

features.name: physical controls
verbatim: there are physical buttons for most functions including the dual zone climate control AC.

features.name: practicality
verbatim: the practicality of the Caretta. This car has all types of charging options right from wireless to USB-A, USB-C and also 12V.

features.name: rear cross traffic alert
verbatim: The rear cross traffic alert is a boon while driving, not so much when reversing from standstill as it keeps applying brakes as people keep walking past.

features.name: rear headrest
verbatim: rear centre passenger gets no head

features.name: rear sun blinds
verbatim: The rear sun blinds ensure privacy and prevent the cabin from heating.

features.name: request sensor
verbatim: There is no request sensor on the co-passenger door, something I missed as I tend to carry a handful of stuff only to realise, I either go to the driver's side and press the request sensor or remove the key from my pocket to unlock the car, quite inconvenient.

features.name: ride quality
verbatim: the Creta is a no-nonsense car which offers a good ride

features.name: safe exit warning
verbatim: the blind view alert and safe exit warning are really useful.

features.name: safety features
verbatim: Hyundai seems to have worked on the safety of this car. Can't wait for it to be crash tested.

features.name: seat comfort
verbatim: The seats are comfortable and the front seats get ventilation which is a big boon in our hot climate.

features.name: seat ventilation
verbatim: the front seats get ventilation which is a big boon in our hot climate.

features.name: spare wheel
verbatim: the spare wheel is smaller size and doesn't get an alloy

features.name: sun visor mirror
verbatim: The driver's side sun visor gets no mirror

features.name: sunroof features
verbatim: why isn't there a dedicated button to open the sunroof blind because you have to be precise in using this button else you end up opening the sunroof when you just want to open the blind.

features.name: touchscreen features
verbatim: The touchscreen is really loaded too, it has a valet mode but I don't know the password. It has a quiet mode but since this is a diesel, the real quiet mode is this, turning off the car.

features.name: tyre pressure monitoring
verbatim: a super useful tyre pressure monitoring system

features.name: wireless charging
verbatim: Wireless charging pad can be turned off so that you can keep your phone here without enabling charging.

features.name: wireless smartphone connectivity
verbatim: no wireless Apple CarPlay or Android Auto

features.name: feature availability
verbatim: I was expecting Hyundai to offer all the features available in the Seltos with the Creta N-line but that never happened, sad.

features.name: interior colour
verbatim: the Creta N-Line has all black interiors which I really like.

features.name: air purifier
verbatim: screen for the air purifier

features.name: automatic wipers
verbatim: rain sensing wipers

features.name: exhaust system
verbatim: visible exhaust

features.name: fog lights
verbatim: fog lamps

features.name: head-up display
verbatim: 8-inch heads-up display

features.name: interior colors
verbatim: multiple interior colour options

features.name: interior lighting
verbatim: multiple ambient light colours which work on the dashboard and doors

features.name: mechanical components
verbatim: the Seltos offers the exact same chassis and powertrain but with a few additional features

features.name: rear headrest
verbatim: rear centre passenger headrest

features.name: reverse lights
verbatim: two reverse lights instead of the single one in the Creta

features.name: wheel size
verbatim: 18-inch wheels

features.name: window controls
verbatim: one-touch up-down for all the windows

features.name: window glass
verbatim: UV-cut glass

features.name: dynamic turn signals
verbatim: Dynamic swipe indicators don't work with hazard, whereas it does in cars like the Scorpio N.

features.name: infotainment system
verbatim: this Creta has more compass menus than what Jeep sells.
